coredata.dc:title: Identification of potential circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks in response to graphene quantum dots in microglia by microarray analysis
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2020 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Inc.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Inc.
coredata.dc:creator: Wu, Tianshu
Li, Yimeng
Liang, Xue
Liu, Xi
Tang, Meng
coredata.dc:description: Along with the increasing application of graphene quantum dots (GQDs) in the fields of biomedicine and neuroscience, it is important to assess the probably adverse effects of GQDs in the central nervous system (CNS) but their underlying toxic mechanisms is still unclear. In this study, we evaluate the molecular mechanisms associated with circular RNAs (circRNAs) of nitrogen-doped GQDs (N-GQDs) and amino-functionalized GQDs (A-GQDs) damaging the cell viability and cellular structure in microglia by an integrative analysis of RNA microarray. The differentially expressed circRNA (DEcircRNAs)-miRNA- differentially expressed mRNA (DEmRNAs) regulatory networks were conducted in BV2 microglial cells treated with 25 µg/mL N-GQDs, 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and 100 µg/mL A-GQDs. Based on that, the protein-coding genes in each ceRNA network were collected to do bio-functional analysis to evaluate signaling pathways that were indirectly mediated by circRNAs. Some pathways that could play indispensable roles in the neurotoxicity of N-GQDs or both two kinds of GQDs were found. Low-dosed N-GQDs exposure mainly induced inflammatory action in microglia, while high-dosed N-GQDs and A-GQDs exposure both affect olfactory transduction and GABAergic synapse. Meanwhile, several classical signaling pathways, including mTOR, ErbB and MAPK, could make diverse contributions to the neurotoxicity of both two kinds of GQDs. These circRNAs could be toxic biomarkers or protective targets in neurotoxicity of GQDs. More importantly, they emphasized the necessity of comprehensive analysis of latent molecular mechanisms through epigenetics approaches in biosafety assessment of graphene-based nanomaterials.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Graphene quantum dot
Circle RNA
BV2 cells
CeRNA
Neurotoxicology

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid fundingbodyid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder sponsoredaccesstype srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure e-component body acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast doctopic grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst misctext orcid primabst pubtype ref

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The characterization of N-GQDs and A-GQDs
RNA extraction and circRNA microarray assay
Analysis of differentially expressed RNA express
Construction of the circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory network
Quantitative real-time reverse-transcription polymerase chain reaction (qRT-PCR) analysis
The toxicity of GQDs in microglia and the identification of DEcircRNAs
Validation of the expression of DEcircRNAs
Construction of circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory network in each treatment group

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The cytotoxicity of N-GQDs and A-GQDs in BV2 cells. (a) The effects of 25 and 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and A-GQDs treatments for 24 h in the cell viability. (b) Representative TEM images showing the ultrastructure of BV2 cells treated with 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and A-GQDs. N indicates nucleus. NE indicates nuclear envelope. M indicates mitochondria. ER indicates endoplasmic reticulum.

Verification of DEcircRNAs identified in the microarray analysis. The relative expression of mmu_circ_0000506 (a), mmu_circ_0001307 (b) and mmu_circ_0001845 (c) based on RT-qPCR analysis was normalized to expression of the reference gene gapdh. Data are presented as means ± SE.

Top 10 hits of GO terms annotations in 3 classifications (Biological Process, Cellular Component, Molecular Function) based on the ceRNA networks in BV2 cells treated with 25 µg/mL N-GQDs (a), 100 µg/mL N-GQDs (b), and 100 µg/mL A-GQDs (c) in comparison with the control (n = 3). The x-axis shows GO terms and the y-axis shows a score value calculated by -log10(P-value).

Top 30 hits of KEGG pathways based on the ceRNA networks in BV2 cells treated with 25 µg/mL N-GQDs (a), 100 µg/mL N-GQDs (b), and 100 µg/mL A-GQDs (c) in comparison with the control (n = 3). The x-axis shows enrichment score and the y-axis shows KEGG pathways.

The visualization of circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks that were predicted by shared DEcircRNAs in BV2 cells treated with N-GQDs (a) and all GQDs (b) in comparison with the control (n = 3). Triangle indicates circRNA, rounded rectangle indicate miRNA, and circle indicates mRNA (Red/Dark one indicates up-regulated mRNA and green/light one indicates down-regulated mRNA). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

Top 10 hits of GO terms annotations in 3 classifications (Biological Process, Cellular Component, Molecular Function) based on the ceRNA networks in BV2 cells treated with N-GQDs (a) and all GQDs (b) in comparison with the control (n = 3). The x-axis shows GO terms and the y-axis shows a score value calculated by -log10(P-value).

Top 30 hits of KEGG pathways based on the ceRNA networks in BV2 cells treated with N-GQDs (a) and all GQDs (b) in comparison with the control (n = 3). The x-axis shows enrichment score and the y-axis shows KEGG pathways.

head.ce:title: Identification of potential circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks in response to graphene quantum dots in microglia by microarray analysis

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding authors at: School of Public Health, Southeast University, Nanjing 210009, PR China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Public Health, Southeast University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Along with the increasing application of graphene quantum dots (GQDs) in the fields of biomedicine and neuroscience, it is important to assess the probably adverse effects of GQDs in the central nervous system (CNS) but their underlying toxic mechanisms is still unclear. In this study, we evaluate the molecular mechanisms associated with circular RNAs (circRNAs) of nitrogen-doped GQDs (N-GQDs) and amino-functionalized GQDs (A-GQDs) damaging the cell viability and cellular structure in microglia by an integrative analysis of RNA microarray. The differentially expressed circRNA (DEcircRNAs)-miRNA- differentially expressed mRNA (DEmRNAs) regulatory networks were conducted in BV2 microglial cells treated with 25 µg/mL N-GQDs, 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and 100 µg/mL A-GQDs. Based on that, the protein-coding genes in each ceRNA network were collected to do bio-functional analysis to evaluate signaling pathways that were indirectly mediated by circRNAs. Some pathways that could play indispensable roles in the neurotoxicity of N-GQDs or both two kinds of GQDs were found. Low-dosed N-GQDs exposure mainly induced inflammatory action in microglia, while high-dosed N-GQDs and A-GQDs exposure both affect olfactory transduction and GABAergic synapse. Meanwhile, several classical signaling pathways, including mTOR, ErbB and MAPK, could make diverse contributions to the neurotoxicity of both two kinds of GQDs. These circRNAs could be toxic biomarkers or protective targets in neurotoxicity of GQDs. More importantly, they emphasized the necessity of comprehensive analysis of latent molecular mechanisms through epigenetics approaches in biosafety assessment of graphene-based nanomaterials.

ce:section.ce:para: As a novel kind of quantum dot, graphene quantum dots (GQDs) are carbon-based zero-dimensional nanomaterials with crystallinity and lattice doping structure. The size of GQDs is usually less than 20 nm, which makes them easily penetrate through biological membranes, including the blood-brain barrier (BBB) (Perini et al., 2020). Even though the study on GQDs is at a relatively early stage, they have presented great promises for the excellent applications in the field of biomedicine and neuroscience, such as bio-imaging, bio-sensing, delivery system and novel drugs. Researchers have found that GQDs could be a future medicine and scope of drugless treatment in the diagnosis and therapeutics of brain tumors and neurodegenerative disorders (Henna and Pramod, 2020; Kim et al., 2018; Su et al., 2020).

GQDs can be doped with abundant functional groups to be suitable for super-molecular recognition, which meets the requirements of different applications as a promising photoluminescence (PL) nanomaterials for fluorescent probes (Mansuriya and Altintas, 2020). Doping nitrogen is a useful and mature chemical modification to greatly improve the photoluminescence quantum yield (PLQY) of GQDs (Deng et al., 2019). Compared to GQDs fabricated through top-down method that have lower than 1% PLQYs, the PLQYs of nitrogen-doped GQDs (N-GQDs) used in this study are quantified more than 20% and they are promising in many fields of biomedicine and neuroscience. Otherwise, amino-functionalized GQDs (A-GQDs) are considered to be suitable for biomedical applications because the amino group has been reported to improve the biocompatibility of GQDs by promoting the linkage between nanomaterials and organic molecules (Mishra and Bhat, 2019).

Right now, several available studies have suggested that GQDs are well biocompatible and non-toxic, especially compared to colloidal semiconductors and graphene oxide (GO) (Henna and Pramod, 2020). According to studies based on exposure of graphene-based nanomaterials, they not only caused neural damages, including neuron death, neurotransmission impairments and behavioral deficits, in experimental animals (Kim et al., 2020; Li et al., 2017), but also induced toxic effects, such as necrosis, increased endothelial permeability and reduced lipid contents in cultured neuronal cells (Luo et al., 2020; Rosas‐Hernandez et al., 2019). Meanwhile, it is a limited number of biosafety evaluations on GQDs, and the findings from toxicological studies of GQDs in neuronal cells or organisms are confined to phenotypes while the alternations in bio-molecules are barely assessed (Li et al., 2017; Zhang et al., 2019; Zheng et al., 2015). Therefore, the toxicity of GQDs needs to be explored for more development and safety before they are permitted in biomedical and neuroscientific applications.

Circular RNA (circRNA) are endogenous non-coding RNAs that are significantly enriched in the brain (You et al., 2015). Several studies have reported the distinctive association of circRNAs with the development of multiple brain disorders (D’Ambra et al., 2019; Ma et al., 2020). CircRNAs have been identified as efficient sponges that sequester specific micro RNAs (miRNAs) to buffer their repression of mRNA targets through the competing endogenous RNAs (ceRNAs) networks (Du et al., 2017; Hansen et al., 2013). Recently, many researchers found that circRNAs not only played important roles in the development of brain disorders (Akhter, 2018; Errichelli et al., 2017), but also regulated toxic responses to GO in experimental animals in certain tissues, including neurons (LifangShi et al., 2019). Therefore, the altered expressions of circRNAs might contribute to the establishment of neuronal disorders caused by GQDs. However, the relationship between the changes in circRNAs profile and neurotoxicity of GQDs is still obscure.

In this study, we detected the altered expressions of circRNAs in BV2 microglial cells treated with N-GQDs with two concentrations and A-GQDs through an omics analysis called RNA microarray. In recent years, it is useful of omics platforms to identify new pathways and mechanisms in nanotoxicity but not visible in conventional testing, especially a lower concentrations. Additionally, the lower interference of omics techniques with nanomaterials would overwhelm the problem of false positive or negative results in conventional toxicity assays triggered by color, fluorescence and chemical activity of nanomaterials (Fröhlich, 2017). Researchers have suggested that possibly influenced biological molecules can be considered to reflect the toxicity of nanomaterials in a rapid way (Cao et al., 2020).

Microglia are residential immunity cells in the CNS and play a critical role in the development and homeostasis of brain. Evaluating the harmful effects of nanoparticles in microglia is an important part of risk assessment of nanoparticles in the CNS. The microglial injuries caused by nanomaterials, especially carbon-based nanoparticles, could result in serious brain disorders (Bussy et al., 2015; Li and Barres, 2018). Therefore, It is necessary to assess the potential biological mechanisms responsible for GQDs-induced adverse outcomes in microglia in order to boost the improvement of GQDs’ neuroscientific applications. Therefore, the construction of the DEcircRNA-miRNA-DEmRNA regulatory network was constructed as well to explore the key circRNAs and relevant signaling pathways that were correlated with GQDs-induced toxicity in microglia.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The characterization of N-GQDs and A-GQDs
ce:section.ce:para: The N-GQDs (Product No. XF241) and A-GQDs (Product No. XF093) used in this study were purchased from XFNANO Materials Tech Co., Ltd. (Nanjing, China) (http://www.xfnano.com). The transmission electron microscope (TEM) images and sizes of them were presented in Fig. S1. Specific data of physicochemical properties of both GQDs were evaluated and showed in the Supporting Information Table S1.

The microglial BV2 cell line was obtained from the Shanghai Cell Research Centre (Shanghai, China). The cell maintained medium was Dulbecco’s modified Eagle’s medium (DMEM) high glucose medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) and 100 U/mL Penicillin and changed every 1–2 days. The cell cultured condition was a humidified atmosphere of 95% O2 and 5% CO2 at 37 °C.

Cell viability was measured using the Cell Counting Kit 8 (CCK8) according to the manufacturer’s protocol. The administrative concentrations of N-GQDs and A-GQDs both were 25 and 100 µg/mL, and the administrative time was 24 h. The CCK8 assay was performed independently in triplicate.

The BV2 cells were fixed in PBS containing 2.5% glutaraldehyde, followed by the standard fixation protocol described in a previous study (Wu et al., 2016). Finally, the ultrathin sections of cells were observed by a TEM.

ce:section.ce:section-title: RNA extraction and circRNA microarray assay
ce:section.ce:para: The Trizol reagent was used to extract total RNAs following with the manufacturer’s protocol. The quantity and quality of RNAs were measured at A260/A280 nm using a NanoDrop ND-2000 spectrophotometer (Thermo Scientific) and an Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100 (Agilent Technologies), respectively. Samples with RNA Integrity Number (RIN) over seven proceeds can be used for microarray detection. The Agilent mouse RNA microarrays were implemented based on several authoritative databases. The process of microarray assay was completed by OE Biotech. Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of differentially expressed RNA express
ce:section.ce:para: The feature extraction (version 10.7.11, Agilent Technologies) was used to obtain raw data that were analyzed using Genespring (version 13.1, Agilent Technologies). The standards of fold change (FC) ≥ 1.5 and p value ≤ 0.05 were set to detect significantly differently expressed mRNA (DEmRNA) or differently expressed circRNA (DEcircRNA).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of the circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory network
ce:section.ce:para: The potential DEcircRNAs of miRNAs were predicted by circular RNA interactome. Additionally, the regulatory relationships between miRNAs and DEmRNAs were predicted using TargetScan database. These DEcircRNA-miRNA pairs and miRNA-DEmRNA pairs were combined to construct the circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory network. The regulatory network was visualized using the Cytoscape software (version 3.7.2).

The predicted function of DEcircRNAs was presented as the enrichments of Gene Ontology (GO) classification and Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes (KEGG) pathways of the target DEmRNAs of mRNAs sponged by DEcircRNAs in BV2 cells treated with GQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Quantitative real-time reverse-transcription polymerase chain reaction (qRT-PCR) analysis
ce:section.ce:para: Equal quantities of total RNA of BV2 cells were used to do qRT-PCR analysis. The qRT-PCR primers were designed by NCBI primer-BLAST based on circBase (http://www.circbase.org/) (Table S2). The relative quantities of circRNA were normalized against the internal reference gene gapdh. Three replicates were conducted for each qRT-PCR analysis.

All data were displayed as the mean±standard deviation (SD). Statistical analysis was performed using GraphPad Prism 6.0 (GraphPad Software, CA, USA). One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was used to determine the statistical significance between control and exposed groups, followed by the Dunnett’s t-test to determine the significance of differences between groups. Probability levels of < 0.05, <0.01 and <0.001 were considered statistically significant.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The toxicity of GQDs in microglia and the identification of DEcircRNAs
ce:section.ce:para: When BV2 cells were treated with 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and A-GQDs for 24 h, the cell viability decreased significantly from the control, while 25 µg/mL GQDs did not change the cell viability ( Fig. 1A). According to TEM images, the malformation of intracellular morphology, including nucleus, mitochondria and endoplasmic reticulum (ER), was observed in BV2 cells caused by 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and A-GQDs (Fig. 1B). The morphological impairments included chromatin condensation, collapse of mitochondrial membrane, broken mitochondrial ridge and ER dilation.

The differences in the expression of circRNAs altered by N-GQDs at different concentrations or A-GQDs in BV2 cells were presented as volcano plots and heatmaps (Fig. S2). The 25 µg/mL N-GQDs exposure only caused 5 DEcircRNAs and 100 µg/mL N-GQDs exposure caused 30 DEcircRNAs in BV2 cells, while A-GQDs induced 35 DEcircRNAs when the exposure concentration was 100 µg/mL (Table S3). Intriguingly, all DEcircRNAs were upregulated and the detailed information of them were presented in Tables S4–S6. Among these upregulated circRNAs, most of them came from chromosome 2 and 4 when exposed to 100 µg/mL A-GQDs, while none of them caused by GQDs came from chromosome 12 and 13 (Fig. S3).

Meanwhile, there are 3 DEcircRNAs both caused by N-GQDs with two administrative concentrations (Table S7), and 14 DEcircRNAs both elicited by 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and A-GQDs in BV2 cells (Table S8). Intriguingly, the shared DEcircRNAs in three GQDs-treatment groups were the three DEcircRNAs both caused by 25 µg/mL and 100 µg/mL N-GQDs, i.e. mmu_circ_0000506, mmu_circ_0001307 and mmu_circ_0001845, which were chosen to corroborate results from the microarray analysis.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Validation of the expression of DEcircRNAs
ce:section.ce:para: The expression of three DEcircRNAs (mmu_circ_0000506, mmu_circ_0001307 and mmu_circ_0001845) caused by GQDs exposure in microarray analysis were verified using the qRT-PCR analysis. Expectedly, N-GQDs and A-GQDs exposure all significantly caused upregulation in expression of the three circRNAs, which was consistent with results from the microarray analysis ( Fig. 2). However, 100 µg/mL A-GQDs exposure caused larger fold changes in the up-regulated expression of mmu_circ_0001307 and mmu_circ_0001845 measured by qRT-PCR analysis than microarray analysis in BV2 cells when the tendency was same (Fig. 2b and c).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory network in each treatment group
ce:section.ce:para: In each GQDs-treatment group, the DEcircRNA-miRNA interactions and miRNA-DEmRNA interactions were integrated to construct circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks, which provided a preliminary insight into the links between the DEcircRNAs and DEmRNAs that were caused by 25 µg/mL N-GQDs, 100 µg/mL N-GQDs or 100 µg/mL A-GQDs (Fig. S4). After that, the DEmRNAs in each regulatory network were enriched into GO terms and KEGG pathways to analyze their potential biological functions. The top 10 enriched GO terms were presented in three classifications, i.e. biological process, cellular component, molecular function, in each GQDs-treatment group. Among terms in biological process, most protein-coding genes probably mediated through DEcircRNAs caused by 25 µg/mL and 100 µg/mL N-GQDs were involved in the cell development and shape and the calcium ion import and transportation, respectively ( Fig. 3a and b), while 100 µg/mL A-GQDs altered the expression of mRNAs associated with circRNA that were involved in wnt signaling pathway (Fig. 3c).

The KEGG analysis showed the top 30 KEGG signaling pathways into which enriched DEmRNAs mediated by DEcircRNAs in BV2 cells treated with N-GQDs at different concentrations or 100 µg/mL A-GQDs. When BV2 cells were exposed to 25 µg/mL N-GQDs, most DEmRNAs were enriched into inflammation-relevant pathways, including TNF, NF-kappaB and IL-17 signaling pathways ( Fig. 4a). Meanwhile, most protein-coding genes in 100 µg/mL N-GQDs and 100 µg/mL A-GQDs were separately clustered in calcium signaling pathway and wnt signaling pathway, respectively (Fig. 4b and c). However, the exposure of N-GQDs and A-GQDs at 100 µg/mL both caused circRNAs associated DEmRNAs to be enriched into olfactory transduction, gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA)ergic synapse and tyrosine metabolism.

The shared three DEcircRNAs, i.e. mmu_circ_0000506, mmu_circ_0001307 and mmu_circ_0001845, and DEmRNAs in two N-GQDs-treatment groups or all three GQDs-treatment groups were employed to specify the key circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory modules in the process of N-GQDs or GQDs without considering chemical modifications causing toxic effects in BV2 cells ( Fig. 5). The largest node that interacted with protein-coding and non-coding RNAs both was mmu_circ_0000506.

Next, GO and KEGG analysis were also performed to evaluate the biological functions of DEmRNAs that shared the same binding miRNAs with DEcircRNAs in the regulatory networks. The results of the top 10 enriched GO terms in biological process showed DEmRNAs mediated by DEcircRNAs that were both altered by N-GQDs no matter the concentrations were associated with cell development and cell contraction ( Fig. 6a). Only 10 KEGG pathways were enriched by shared DEmRNAs in N-GQDs-treated BV2 cells ( Fig. 7a).

Meanwhile, the top 10 GO terms in which were enriched by DEmRNAs mediated through DEcircRNAs that were both caused by N-GQDs and A-GQDs indicated the role of GQDs in the regulation of DNA and RNA transcription (Fig. 6b). Top 30 KEGG pathways included several classical signaling pathways, such as mTOR, ErbB, TNF, PI3K-Akt, MAPK and Ras (Fig. 7b).

Even though GQDs is a relatively novel type of fluorescent nanocrystals in recent years, their unique optical properties, small dimensions and molecular-like biocompatibility makes GQDs highly suitable in biomedical and neuroscience applications. Meanwhile, the increasing exposure of GQDs to the public and the environment originated from rising applications indicated the importance of their biosafety assessment. Since microglia play important roles in the normal function of CNS, the investigation of toxic effects and potential mechanisms of GQDs in microglia would provide significant information in the neurotoxicity of GQDs. There are studies suggesting genetic damages caused by GQDs in cultured cell lines (Li and Barres, 2018; Li et al., 2018; Wang et al., 2015; Xu et al., 2019; Yu et al., 2019). In this study, the role of circRNAs in the GQDs-caused neurotoxic effects in BV2 microglial cells were assessed by an integrative RNA microarray and bioinformatics analysis.

According to some administrated doses reported to use for biomedical applications (Deng et al., 2018; Li and Barres, 2018; Li et al., 2018; Xu et al., 2019) and our previous study (Wu et al., 2020), the treatment concentrations of 25 and 100 µg/mL were used here to assess the cytotoxicity of GQDs in microglia at the first time. Apart from the decreased cell viability, the impairments of nuclei, mitochondria and ER directly indicated the toxic effects of N-GQDs and A-GQDs in BV2 cells when the treatment concentration reached to 100 µg/mL, while the exposure of N-GQDs and A-GQDs at 25 µg/mL did not cause obviously cell damages. Therefore, these two concentrations were selected for the microarray to obtain sensitive non-coding RNAs, like circRNAs, under the circumstances of non-toxicity or slight cytotoxicity of N-GQDs. Meantime, only 100 µg/mL A-GQDs were included into the microarray analysis since A-GQDs seems causing milder effects than N-GQDs in terms of cell viability and intracellular morphology.

According to the results of microarray analysis, the DEcircRNAs caused by GQDs were all up-regulated in BV2 cells, and only three circRNAs (mmu_circ_0000506, mmu_circ_0001307 and mmu_circ_0001845) were significantly dysregulated by N-GQDs and A-GQDs no matter which administrative concentration is. The data indicated not only the involvement of circRNAs in the process of GQDs causing toxic effects, but also the specific toxic targets of GQDs. As the circRNAs have been reported to act as miRNA sponges to counteract miRNA mediated repression of mRNA (Hansen et al., 2013), the circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory modules following with bioinformatics analysis were conducted to evaluate the potential molecular mechanisms of GQDs’ neurotoxicity.

The GO terms and KEGG pathways enriched by DEmRNAs from the DEcircRNA-miRNA-DEmRNA regulatory networks in 25 µg/mL N-GQDs treatment group indicated exclusive signals in the early biological process of N-GQDs in BV2 cells without remarkable cytotoxicity. The findings suggested that N-GQDs exposure at the non-cytotoxic concentration could affect the cell development and cause slight inflammatory actions. On the contrary, the bioinformatic analysis based on the circRNAs exclusive altered by 100 µg/mL N-GQDs provided a hint that N-GQDs exposure at a high concentration could change the calcium ion import and transportation. The significantly enriched GO terms and KEGG pathways for the DEmRNAs are highly related to wnt signaling pathway in BV2 cells treated with 100 µg/mL A-GQDs. Of note, the KEGG analysis suggested that both N-GQDs and A-GQDs could alter the expression of circRNAs that participate in olfactory transduction, GABAergic synapse and tyrosine metabolism.

In this study, the similar mechanistic pathways of N-GQDs without considering the exposure concentration and damages caused by both N-GQDs and A-GQDs in microglia were evaluated through analyzing those shared DEcircRNAs and DEmRNAs. The findings suggested the circRNA mmu_circ_0000506 was the largest node in all circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks. The gene symbol of mmu_circ_0000506 is Myst4. Researchers have found circMyst4 was one of highly brain-specifically-expressed circRNAs as the predominant isoform (Rybak-Wolf et al., 2015). Since circRNAs were extraordinarily enriched in mammalian brain and well conserved in sequence, their altered expressions caused by GQDs probably result in severe health consequences in the CNS.

When conducting biological function analysis based on ceRNA networks, the GABAergic synapse and olfactory transduction are significantly enriched in two N-GQDs-treatment groups, which indicated these two pathways could play indispensable roles in N-GQDs causing cytotoxicity in microglia. Researchers have identified GABA abnormalities in the brains of patients having neurodegenerative disorders, like Alzheimer's disease (Ambrad Giovannetti and Fuhrmann, 2019). Olfactory transduction participated in the natural olfactory systems of mammals (Cave et al., 2019).

Otherwise, the KEGG pathway analysis based on shared DEcircRNAs altered by N-GQDs and A-GQDs suggested that many classical signaling pathways that have been reported in many nanotoxicological studies might be involved in the toxicity of GQDs in microglia as well. Among these pathways regulated by DEcircRNAs whose expression levels were changed by GQDs exposure, the signaling pathways of mechanistic target of rapamycin (mTOR) and erythroblastosis oncogene B (ErbB) were relatively important. The mTOR signaling pathway in the brain takes part in many biological processes, such as glucose metabolism, cell growth, autophagy and mitochondrial function, which play a complex role in the neurodegenerative disorders (Borst et al., 2019). A growing number of studies highlight that disturbance in ErbB signaling affects multiple signaling pathways, including mTOR, PI3K-Akt, MAPK, and calcium, which are regulatory players in the development and function of CNS, including microglia (Kataria et al., 2019; Xiang et al., 2015). The influence of GQDs in these pathways through circRNAs in microglia could result in severe consequences in the CNS since microglia are key regulators neurodegenerative and neuroinflammatory diseases.

Recently, some researchers suggested that it is important to consider a heterogeneous and representative set of cell types for the molecular regulation induced by carbon-based nanomaterials (Scala et al., 2018). As we know, no microglia cell lines certainly come without faults, so the BV2 cell line is frequently used as an alternative of primary microglia owing to a shorter preparation time and its homogeneous population across biological experiments (Stansley et al., 2012). This microarray analysis based on BV2 cells presented a stable and repeatable result regarding to circRNAs altered by GQDs. However, the immortalized cells are subject to genetic drift and morphology changes. Some researchers have found some specific signaling pathways and functions substantially differed between primary microglia and BV2 cells (He et al., 2018), which reminds us to be cautious about the findings and be necessary to testify them.

Another limitation of microarray analysis based on cultured cells was similar to the in vitro studies. The in vitro high-throughput screening is necessary for toxicity assessment of nanomaterials, but the nanotoxicological studies based on the conventional 2D cell cultures may ignore some important issues (Cao et al., 2020). Therefore, it is important to develop advanced tissue-based cell cultures. A recent study reported to use 3D brain organoids to assess the neurotoxicity of multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) (Jiang et al., 2020). If plausible, it would be more reliable to combine multiple biological models to assess the toxicity of nanomaterials.

When toxicogenomic approaches were used to identify unique gene expression profiles that providing mechanistic information, the relevance of specific epigenomic effects, such as alterations of miRNA or circRNA expressions, triggered by carbon-based nanomaterials exposure was also discussed in the identification of affected regulatory pathways and biomarkers (Ventura et al., 2018). Even in the brain of a model animal called zebrafish, the omics analysis provided insights into the actual and highly influential molecular mechanisms that are relevant to organelle dysfunction or cellular disruption, underlying the toxicity of GO (Sun et al., 2019). Taken findings in this study into consideration, it seems carbon or graphene-based nanomaterials exposure could cause obvious epigenomic effects in living bodies without inducing severe healthy consequences.

Our study reveals for the first time that circRNAs are dysregulated in microglia treated with GQDs. Bioinformatics analysis indicated several signaling pathways and biological molecules associated with the DEcircRNAs that might play pivotal roles in the process of GQDs inducing neurotoxic effects. However, it still needs to uncover the underlying molecular mechanisms of circRNAs in the interaction between GQDs and neuronal cells in further, because those circRNAs are identified on the basis of computational biology. As we know, the ultimate aim of assessing the toxicity of GQDs is to find low-toxicity but good-efficient GQDs in wide applications. Identification of sensitive biomarkers influenced by GQDs through microarray analysis could be a first step to achieve the best balance point. Unfortunately, this study has not reported the specific regulation or interaction of circRNAs in the signaling pathways that are involved in GQDs inducing toxic effects, which requires further exploration.

In this study, the circRNA-miRNA-mRNA regulatory networks were identified through an integrative RNA microarray approach to explore potential molecular mode of action in microglia treated with N-GQDs with different doses or A-GQDs. As we know, circRNAs acting as sponges of miRNAs negatively regulated by protein-coding genes could take part in signaling transduction. Therefore, the following bio-functional analysis based on ceRNA networks revealed DEcircRNAs that are capable of participating in diverse biological processes by targeting corresponding mRNAs to be involved in the neurotoxicity of GQDs as pivotal knots. Taken together, The circRNA-miRNA-mRNA axes influenced by GQDs indicated the indispensable role of non-coding RNAs in neuronal cell damage responded to GQDs except considering traditional signal-net regulations. In further study, loss- and gain-of-function experiments based on the findings need to uncover the underlying mechanisms of circRNAs in the adverse effects of GQDs in the CNS.

Tianshu Wu: Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing - original draft, Writing - review & editing. Yimeng Li: Validation, Data curation. Xue Liang: Validation, Data curation. Xi Liu: Validation. Meng Tang: Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: Authors of this article declare that there are no conflicts of competing financial interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China (grant numbers 21876026, 31671034), Natural Science Foundation of Jiangsu Province (grant number BK20180371), Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (grant numbers 2242020K40192) and Supporting Program of Southeast University Zhishan Young Scholar (grant number 2242020R40140).

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2020.111672.
